# Design Libraries by Hal

> Three ratio-based Figma design token libraries (Golden Ratio, Typographic Ratio, Base 10) exported as DTCG JSON, with an npm package that compiles them into CSS custom properties and a Tailwind v4 theme.

## Overview

Each library answers the question "where do the values in your design system come from?" using a different mathematical foundation. All three share the same token structure ‚Äî 10 DTCG-format JSON files covering colours, spacing, typography, sizes, effects, and responsive breakpoints ‚Äî but derive their scales from different ratios.

- **Golden Ratio** ‚Äî œÜ = 1.618, applied from a base of 8px. Fibonacci-like progression: 0, 1, 2, 4, 8, 13, 21, 34, 55, 89, 144, 232, 376, 608.
- **Typographic Ratio** ‚Äî classical type scale accumulated over centuries of print and screen typography.
- **Base 10** ‚Äî round numbers in multiples of 10 with mid-steps for finer control.

## NPM Package

The npm package `@hferello/design-libraries-npm` transforms the DTCG token JSON into three CSS files per library using Style Dictionary v4.

Install from GitHub Packages:

```
npm install @hferello/design-libraries-npm --registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
```

### Usage

For a Tailwind v4 project, import the tailwind-theme.css for your chosen library:

```css
@import "@hferello/design-libraries-npm/golden-ratio/tailwind-theme.css";
```

For a non-Tailwind project, import tokens.css instead:

```css
@import "@hferello/design-libraries-npm/golden-ratio/tokens.css";
```

Replace `golden-ratio` with `typographic-ratio` or `base-10-ratio` as needed.

### Output Files

Each library produces three CSS files:

- `primitives.css` ‚Äî raw palette values in `:root` (number scale, colour hues, transparent overlays)
- `tokens.css` ‚Äî semantic layer with `var(--primitive-*)` alias references, `.dark` overrides, and responsive `@media` breakpoints
- `tailwind-theme.css` ‚Äî Tailwind v4 entry point splitting tokens into `@theme static` (generates utility classes) and `:root` (plain CSS variables)

### CSS Variable Namespaces

Tokens inside `@theme static` that generate Tailwind utility classes:

| Category | CSS namespace | Utility examples |
|---|---|---|
| Spacing (11 values) | `--spacing-*` | `p-tight`, `gap-section`, `m-apart` |
| Grid spatial | `--spacing-grid-*` | `gap-grid-gutter`, `p-grid-margin` |
| Font sizes (7 values) | `--text-*` | `text-xs`, `text-base`, `text-2xl` |
| Border radius (6 values) | `--radius-*` | `rounded-soft`, `rounded-pill` |
| Blur (4 values) | `--blur-*` | `blur-soft`, `blur-void` |
| All colours | `--color-*` | `bg-neutral-base`, `text-fg-neutral-aa` |

Plain CSS variables (access via arbitrary values in Tailwind):

| Category | Variable | Tailwind access |
|---|---|---|
| Grid columns | `--grid-columns` | `grid-cols-[var(--grid-columns)]` |
| Border weight (4 values) | `--border-*` | `border-[length:var(--border-hairline)]` |
| Opacity (5 values) | `--opacity-*` | `opacity-[var(--opacity-high)]` |

### Spacing Name Mapping

Figma's descriptive token names map to concise CSS names:

| Figma token name | CSS variable | Value pattern |
|---|---|---|
| Flush | `--spacing-flush` | 0 |
| Tightly related, one unit | `--spacing-tight` | scale step 1 |
| Clearly paired, slight breath | `--spacing-paired` | scale step 2 |
| Same group, distinct items | `--spacing-grouped` | scale step 3 |
| Loose group, breathing room | `--spacing-loose` | scale step 4 |
| Distinct sections, clear gap | `--spacing-section` | scale step 5 |
| Independent, different context | `--spacing-independent` | scale step 6 |
| New section | `--spacing-region` | scale step 7 |
| Clear visual break between zones | `--spacing-zone` | scale step 8 |
| Unrelated, structural separation | `--spacing-divide` | scale step 9 |
| Very far apart | `--spacing-apart` | scale step 10 |

### Font Size Mapping

| Figma name | CSS variable |
|---|---|
| XSmall | `--text-xs` |
| Small | `--text-sm` |
| Regular | `--text-base` |
| Medium | `--text-md` |
| Large | `--text-lg` |
| XLarge | `--text-xl` |
| XXLarge | `--text-2xl` |

### Sizes Mapping

Border radius: `--radius-sharp`, `--radius-subtle`, `--radius-soft`, `--radius-rounded`, `--radius-pill`, `--radius-circular`

Border weight: `--border-hairline`, `--border-defined`, `--border-bold`, `--border-graphic`

Blur: `--blur-soft`, `--blur-hazy`, `--blur-foggy`, `--blur-void`

Opacity: `--opacity-full`, `--opacity-high`, `--opacity-medium`, `--opacity-low`, `--opacity-faint`

### Colour Naming Convention

Semantic colours follow this pattern:

- Surface neutrals: `--color-neutral-{level}` (base, faint, subtle, etc.)
- Surface hues: `--color-{hue}-{level}` (blue-base, green-bold, etc.)
- Foreground neutrals: `--color-fg-neutral-{level}` (aa, aaa, dark, etc.)
- Foreground hues: `--color-fg-{hue}-{level}`
- Icon colours: `--color-icon-{name}` (subtle, base, bold, inverse, blue, etc.)
- Overlays: `--color-overlay-{pct}` (0, 8, 12, 16, 24, 36, 48, 60, 72, 96)

Dark mode overrides are scoped to `.dark` or `[data-theme="dark"]`.

Primitive colours: `--primitive-color-{hue}-{step}` (e.g. `--primitive-color-neutral-100`, `--primitive-color-blue-500`)

### Responsive Behaviour

The package generates range-based media queries:

- Desktop: default (no media query)
- Tablet: `@media (min-width: 768px) and (max-width: 1199px)`
- Mobile: `@media (max-width: 767px)`

Font sizes and grid settings adjust per breakpoint automatically.

## Token Source Files

Each library folder contains 10 DTCG-format JSON files:

- `Colours.Light.tokens.json` ‚Äî semantic colours for light mode
- `Colours.Dark.tokens.json` ‚Äî semantic colours for dark mode
- `Primatives.tokens.json` ‚Äî raw colour palette and number scale
- `Spacing.Positive.tokens.json` ‚Äî positive spacing scale
- `Spacing.Negative.tokens.json` ‚Äî negative spacing scale (Figma-internal)
- `Sizes.tokens.json` ‚Äî border radius and border weight
- `Effects.tokens.json` ‚Äî blur and opacity levels
- `Responsive.Desktop.tokens.json` ‚Äî font sizes, grid settings (desktop)
- `Responsive.Tablet.tokens.json` ‚Äî font sizes, grid settings (tablet)
- `Responsive.Mobile.tokens.json` ‚Äî font sizes, grid settings (mobile)

## Links

- [NPM Package (GitHub)](https://github.com/hferello/design-libraries-npm): Source code and build pipeline
- [Token Reference Site](https://hferello.github.io/design-libraries/): Visual reference for all token values
- [Golden Ratio Figma Library](https://lnkd.in/e9gqH7Np): Free on Figma Community
- [Typographic Ratio Figma Library](https://lnkd.in/efJe88uR): Free on Figma Community
- [Base 10 Figma Library](https://lnkd.in/eqNstcZr): Free on Figma Community
